questions.question: A heap is best described as:
choices: An organized structure (tree) with priority rules

questions.question: In a Max-Heap, which value is at the top?
In a Min-Heap, which value is at the top?
In a Max-Heap tree, what do you call the node above other nodes?
In the story, building a heap represents:
If Faith puts less important items first instead of the most important ones, what structure does this resemble?
choices: A min-heap when a max-heap is required

questions.question: What is the goal when building a heap?
choices: To organize the items so they follow the heap rule
To reverse the order of all items

questions.question: What is the main goal of Heap Sort?
If the goal is to sort items in ASCENDING order using Heap Sort, what kind of heap are you making?
If the goal is to sort items in DESCENDING order using Heap Sort, what kind of heap are you making?
In Heap Sort, how are items placed into their final order?
What makes Heap Sort different from simple sorting methods?
choices: It uses a heap structure to organize items

questions.question: As more items are placed correctly, what happens to the unsorted group?
When does Heap Sort finish?
choices: When all items are placed in order

questions.question: What is the main goal of the QuickSort algorithm?
In QuickSort, what is a pivot?
choices: A chosen number used to compare other numbers

questions.question: What step in Quick Sort divides numbers into groups around the pivot?
After grouping the numbers around a pivot, what happens next?
choices: The process repeats on the smaller groups

questions.question: What do you do with numbers smaller than the pivot?
choices: Put them in the Less Than group

questions.question: What is the final result after repeating the grouping process many times?
choices: The numbers become sorted in order
